rag_rules: Place component type contracts (assumptions, guarantees) in `annex agree { ... };` blocks within the component type.
Place implementation-level bindings (assignments) in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts should reference visible component ports and data types from the AADL model.
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names when defining ranges.
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in guarantees; avoid implementation-only paths in component type contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq <variable_name> : <type> = <expression>;` to define variables for range checks.
Ensure AGREE expression types match AADL port types (bool, int, real).
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) directly as AGREE variables.
Avoid range comparisons or equality checks on Boolean or opaque data ports without a numeric scalar representation.
